title: House spirit shines at York High interhouse athletics
bodyText: [{"type":"contributor","text":"Hugo Redelinghuys","contributorName":"Hugo Redelinghuys","contributorDate":"Thursday, 29 January 2026, 12:39"},{"type":"intro_bold","text":"The boys' U16 high jump produced one of the fiercest duels of the meeting, as Zian Rheeder and Konrad Punt were locked in an intense battle."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"INTERHOUSE ATHLETICS - York High School's interhouse athletics meeting at Eden Technical High's sports-grounds on Monday delivered a vibrant mix of excellent performances, fierce competition and unforgettable house spirit.","html":"INTERHOUSE ATHLETICS - York High School's interhouse athletics meeting at Eden Technical High's sports-grounds on Monday delivered a vibrant mix of excellent performances, fierce competition and unforgettable house spirit."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Despite overcast conditions, the atmosphere was anything but dull. From the opening events, the stands came alive with colour, song and relentless encouragement as the various houses rallied behind their athletes.","html":"Despite overcast conditions, the atmosphere was anything but dull. From the opening events, the stands came alive with colour, song and relentless encouragement as the various houses rallied behind their athletes."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The electric vibe in the pavilion proved to be a powerful motivator, pushing competitors to dig deep and produce some outstanding performances on the track and in the field."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The highlight of the day came in the girls' U17 400m, in which Lilly Joubert powered home to set a new record of 66.03s, stamping her authority on the event and providing one of the meeting's standout moments.","html":"The highlight of the day came in the girls' U17 400m, in which Lilly Joubert powered home to set a new record of 66.03s, stamping her authority on the event and providing one of the meeting's standout moments."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Another eye-catching display was delivered by the 15-year-old Madison Smith, who dominated the girls' U15 100m sprint in fine style. She came tantalisingly close to adding a second headline perfor-mance when she attempted to break the high jump record and sailed over at 1.4m.","html":"Another eye-catching display was delivered by the 15-year-old Madison Smith, who dominated the girls' U15 100m sprint in fine style. She came tantalisingly close to adding a second headline perfor-mance when she attempted to break the high jump record and sailed over at 1.4m."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The boys' U16 high jump produced one of the fiercest duels of the meeting, as Zian Rheeder and Konrad Punt were locked in an intense battle. Jump for jump, the pair pushed each other to higher levels before Zian eventually emerged victorious.","html":"The boys' U16 high jump produced one of the fiercest duels of the meeting, as Zian Rheeder and Konrad Punt were locked in an intense battle. Jump for jump, the pair pushed each other to higher levels before Zian eventually emerged victorious."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Several sprinters also caught the eye with their sharp, confident performances, including Alice Malinga, Lea Schmidt, Dawid Lamprecht, Simcilele Ngwemesha, Sifiso Mncube, Hadley Botha, Talia Soldin and Earl Johnson, all of whom showcased impressive speed and composure under pressure.","html":"Several sprinters also caught the eye with their sharp, confident performances, including Alice Malinga, Lea Schmidt, Dawid Lamprecht, Simcilele Ngwemesha, Sifiso Mncube, Hadley Botha, Talia Soldin and Earl Johnson, all of whom showcased impressive speed and composure under pressure."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"In the field events, Christian van Zyl won the boys' U17 discus, while Kieran Harding soared above the competition to claim victory in the girls' open high jump."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"When the final points were tallied, House Tudor emerged as the overall winners with 815.5 points, narrowly edging out House Stuart on 795 points. House Lancaster finished a close third with 769.5 points, followed by House Windsor on 648 points.","html":"When the final points were tallied, House Tudor emerged as the overall winners with 815.5 points, narrowly edging out House Stuart on 795 points. House Lancaster finished a close third with 769.5 points, followed by House Windsor on 648 points."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Stuart's unwavering enthusiasm and vocal support earned them the well-deserved spirit award.","html":"Stuart's unwavering enthusiasm and vocal support earned them the well-deserved spirit award."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The junior victor ludorum title was awarded to David Lamprecht, and Alice Malinga received the junior victrix ludorum. The senior victor ludorum went to Rowan Robertson and the senior victrix ludorum title was awarded to Lilly Joubert.","html":"The junior victor ludorum title was awarded to David Lamprecht, and Alice Malinga received the junior victrix ludorum. The senior victor ludorum went to Rowan Robertson and the senior victrix ludorum title was awarded to Lilly Joubert."}]

House Lancaster finished a close third with 769.5 points, followed by House Windsor on 648 points."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Stuart's unwavering enthusiasm and vocal support earned them the well-deserved spirit award.","html":"Stuart's unwavering enthusiasm and vocal support earned them the well-deserved spirit award."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The junior victor ludorum title was awarded to David Lamprecht, and Alice Malinga received the junior victrix ludorum. images.alt: House spirit shines at York High interhouse athletics

author: Hugo Redelinghuys
teaser: The boys' U16 high jump produced one of the fiercest duels of the meeting, as Zian Rheeder and Konrad Punt were locked in an intense battle.
featuredCaption: Zian Rheeder clears the bar to win the boys' U16 high jump in a fierce duel with Konrad Punt. Photo: Hugo Redelinghuys
